summary: Surah Ad-Dukhan describes the blessed night when the Qur’an was revealed and warns of a Day when the sky will bring forth a visible smoke as a sign of punishment for the disbelievers. It recounts the story of Pharaoh and the rescue of the Children of Israel, emphasizing Allah's mercy for the believers and severe punishment for the disbelievers. The surah contrasts the fate of the pious in Paradise with the torment of sinners in Hell, and affirms the truth and purpose of creation.
